company.name: Replicate
company.notes: AI model hosting (Flux, LTX — your stack). Distributed. Direct fit.

research.status_summary: Replicate was acquired by Cloudflare on December 1, 2025 for $57.4M and is now operating as a fully integrated part of Cloudflare's AI Platform team — no longer an independent company.
research.hiring_policy: Replicate's team has merged into Cloudflare's AI Platform org; new hiring is through Cloudflare (a publicly traded US company), which historically sponsors visas and has global remote roles, but EOR/direct Replicate-brand hiring is no longer applicable. No standalone Replicate job postings are currently live on YC, a16z, or their own careers page.
recent_news: Acquisition closed December 1, 2025: Cloudflare paid $57.4M cash for all outstanding Replicate shares, per Cloudflare's SEC 10-Q filing (Q1 2026).

Active integration in progress (Apr–May 2026): Cloudflare's AI Platform blog confirmed the Replicate team 'no longer considers itself a separate team,' with all Replicate models being migrated onto AI Gateway and Workers AI infrastructure.

Replicate's Cog containerization technology is now powering Cloudflare's 'bring your own model' feature for Workers AI, and fine-tuning capabilities are being built into Workers AI using Replicate's expertise — announced during Cloudflare's April 2026 Agents Week.
research.growth_signal: Post-acquisition, Replicate's model catalog (50,000+ models including Flux, LTX, SD) is being absorbed into Cloudflare's globally distributed edge network, giving it massive distribution leverage but removing it as a standalone hiring/growth target.
research.fit_for_lior: Replicate's platform is Lior's direct infrastructure stack (Flux, LTX Video), but as a Cloudflare subsidiary it is no longer a hireable independent entity — the relevant opportunity is now at Cloudflare itself, where Replicate's AI model tooling and creative-AI developer focus lives inside the Workers AI / AI Gateway product surface.
